prompts: /plan_adw "create a new Facebook Marketplace Assistant specifically for the Real Estate category. build the frontend in the apps/frontend folder, the backend in the apps/backend folder and the Google Chrome Extension in the app/plugin folder. The backend will use Convex.dev for auth and storage. The frontend will be react and typescript. We prefer to use TypeScript and NestJS for our project. The primary use will be a Google Chrome Extension that the user will use to select properties from the websites: (mls.ca, mls.com, zillow.com) to choose listings that will get scraped and posted to the facebook marketplace for the currently logged in fb user account. We should be able to choose saved lists of properties from the real estate websites and scrape the properites listed in each list, add it into our users account. this will include ALL the data that is normally available from the listing details including the images and videos. From the users account which the can view in the chrome extension, the user will select from a list of properties in card view or list view that they want to scrape the details for. Once the details are scrapped we should be able to choose which ad posting templates we should use from a pre-defined list of posting templates. The AI should then write a posting description and title based on the template selected, and create a draft posting in the users FB Marketplace account including the images and videos. This is the main auto-posting feature of the extension. The extension should also allow the user to chose a property from the real estate sites by clicking on a button that will let the scrape the listing they are viewing. We need to inject a [Post to FB] button into the webpages of the real estate sites for the pages that show the list of properties or the property details or a search result listing. We track which listing get posted into our system for scraping. Once it is in our system the button should show [Repost to Facebook] because we already have it in the system. If we delete if from the system then it can return to [Post to FB]. Use a different color for each. When a real estate listing in fb marketplace get published we need to track it so that we can have an AI Agent respond to inquries and conversations only for the properties we have posted with the marketplace tool, only those conversations, not ALL conversations. the plugin should use a side panel for its interface, have user authentication controlled by convex. We also should have a more full featured web version they user can log into to manage their postings. Since we want this to be an SaaS offering for Real Estate agencies, we want to be able to manage all the team members for an office in the web version. So a user can view and manage their own, and a manager can manage the whole team. The App will need a catchy name. We will use Stripe to handle subscriptions. Ask me questions if you need to help. Make this app amazing and easy to use for non-technical people who are Real Estate agents."

The user should be already logged into the websites for realtor.ca or realtor.com or zillow so we can search, filter, save, and view property listings and saved property lists. Otherwise we need some kind of intergrations section where we connect and save credentials to those sites that we support. The first site we will support is realtor.ca, then realtor.com, then zillow.

We want to have good onboarding to make it as frictionless as possible for  users to sign up and start posting, so the free posting will use our corporate llm account. All the "magic" ai goodness happens in the backend, so we send the data regarding the property listing, the template and other details to our backend api endpoint which will create the listing for free if it is qualifed for it or use the users llm keys. Maybe we only send it back to our end if they qualify for free listing generation and they don't have llm keys setup.

Remaining Questions for You:
  1. Do you have Facebook Developer app access for Marketplace integration? - no, but we can get it. prefer to use direct script injection.
  2. Which analytics provider do you prefer (PostHog, Amplitude, or Mixpanel)? no preference, maybe go with posthog
  3. What's the target launch date? ASAP
  4. Do you have design assets or should we create from scratch? create from scratch, we'll also need a landing page and marketing collatoral
  5. Should the Agency tier team limit be 10 members? Yes
  6. Free trial period for paid tiers? no
  7. White-label options for large agencies? yes

Updated Subscription Tiers:
  - Free: 100 scrapes, 5 posts, AI included, no credit card
  - Pro ($250): 1500 scrapes a month, 500 posts a month, AI included + option for own keys
  - Agency ($997): Unlimited + team (25 users), analytics, Slack, AI chat agent

No additional real estate sites for now. When we find out the primary real estate listing sites in the UK, Australia, New Zealand, and others, we need to create an implementation schedule to add them.

In the future we want to include some kind of upsells for training and other real estate specific needs. So some kind of way to offer that via the app or website would be nice, we should plan for that as it is on the road-map, but not required for the MVP launch.
An affiliate program would be useful as well, so consider how we can include that as part of our marketing and how we would need to track that for signups and purchases.

We want clear insights into how users are actually using the product, how many search, scrapes, posts created, posts published, how long they stay active, how many responses per conversion (conversation messages) and other relevant kpis for the product.

here is an existing first draft fb realestate poster we made previously, perhaps there is something in there to use for our existing project, possibly layout, or fb script injection, or real estate site injection, or something else : /home/andre/batcave/fb-real-estate-poster-v0.3
